title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: You have been asked to write a report for your local council about a library that is underused. You should explain why people are not using it and suggest ways to make it more popular.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Revitalising the Westside Library: A Report

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to investigate the declining visitor numbers at Westside Library and to propose actionable strategies to re-engage the local community. My findings are based on interviews with residents and local business owners.

Reasons for Low Attendance
The consensus among residents is that the library feels outdated. The interior is poorly lit, and the book collection has not been significantly updated in several years. Furthermore, the lack of modern digital facilities is a major deterrent; in an age where high-speed internet is essential, the library’s two functional computers are woefully inadequate. Finally, the restricted opening hours mean that working professionals and students find it difficult to visit.

Proposed Improvements
To address these issues, I suggest the following measures:
- Modernisation of Facilities: The installation of a high-speed Wi-Fi network and a 'digital lounge' with tablets and e-readers would attract a younger demographic.
- Community Events: Transforming the library into a community hub by hosting weekly workshops, such as coding clubs or creative writing groups, would encourage regular visits.
- Cafe Installation: A small coffee shop within the building would create a more welcoming, social atmosphere.
- Extended Hours: Opening until 8:00 PM at least twice a week would accommodate those with full-time jobs.

Conclusion
Westside Library has the potential to be a vibrant asset once again. By embracing technology and social interaction, the council can ensure the library remains relevant in the 21st century.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt: You have seen an advertisement for a volunteer position at a summer camp for children in another country. Write an email to the organiser, explaining why you are interested, what skills you have, and asking about the conditions of the work.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Mr. Thompson,

I am writing to express my strong interest in the volunteer camp counsellor position at 'Sunshine Summer Camp', as advertised on your website. I have always been passionate about youth development and am eager to contribute to your team this summer.

I believe my background makes me an ideal candidate for this role. Currently, I am a university student majoring in Education, and I have two years of experience working as a part-time tutor for children aged 8 to 12. This has helped me develop excellent communication skills and the ability to remain calm under pressure. Furthermore, I am a certified lifeguard and an avid guitar player, both of which I believe would be valuable assets for camp activities.

One of the reasons I am particularly drawn to this position is the opportunity to work in a multicultural environment. I am keen to learn about different perspectives on education while sharing my own cultural heritage with the campers.

I would be grateful if you could provide some further information regarding the conditions of the volunteer work. Specifically, I would like to know if accommodation and meals are provided on-site. Additionally, could you please clarify the exact dates of the placement and whether any specific training is required prior to arrival?

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the possibility of discussing this further with you.

Yours sincerely,
Elena Rossi

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen this post on a travel website: 'We want reviews of city tours you have been on. Tell us what the tour consisted of, what the guide was like, and whether it was good value for money.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Berlin by Night: An Underground Perspective

Last month, I joined the 'Berlin Neon Lights' walking tour, and it was easily the highlight of my trip to the German capital. Far from the typical historical marches past monuments, this tour focuses on the city's vibrant street art and alternative culture.

The tour began at 8:00 PM in the Kreuzberg district. Over the course of three hours, we explored hidden courtyards, abandoned industrial spaces converted into galleries, and some of the most famous mural sites in the city. The itinerary was perfectly paced, allowing enough time for photographs without feeling rushed.

Our guide, Stefan, was exceptional. As a local street artist himself, he provided an insider’s view that a standard history book simply couldn't offer. His anecdotes about the origins of specific graffiti pieces and the legal battles between artists and the city were fascinating. He was also incredibly patient, answering every question with infectious enthusiasm and a great sense of humour.

At €25 per person, the tour represents outstanding value for money. Not only did we see parts of Berlin that we never would have found on our own, but we also received a list of local bar and club recommendations that proved to be gold dust. 

I would highly recommend this tour to anyone who wants to see the 'real' Berlin. It’s an eye-opening, gritty, and thoroughly entertaining evening that is worth every cent. Just make sure to wear comfortable shoes!
